license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8d9hs96
title: Massively parallel long-read sequencing of single cell RNA isoforms
author: Jafar S Jabbari

steps_raw.instruction: Follow the 10x Genomics user guide for Single Cell Gene Expression (v3) or Single Cell Immune Profiling (v1 Chemistry) kits for GEM Generation and Barcoding for the required number of cells. NOTE: This protocol is also compatible with Next Gem single cell 3’ (v3.1 Chemistry) and Single Cell Immune Profiling (v1.1 Chemistry) and also feature barcoding.

After transferring GEMs perform GEM-RT Incubation according to the user guide. NOTE: In some experiments we have extended RT time up to 2 hours to potentially increase the number of longer transcripts and have not observed any adverse effect. However, we have not systematically investigated if increased incubation time results in higher number of longer transcripts.
Prepare Sub-Sampling Cleanup Mix: Add 2.5 µl Additive A to 97.5 µl EB (v1 chemistry) Or Add 2 µl Reducing Agent B to 98 µl EB (v3 chemistry).
Transfer 125 µl Recovery Regent into a fresh well of a 8-strip tube.
Dispense 63 µl of Sub-Sampling Cleanup Mix into well containing Recovery Reagent.

Transfer 10-20 µl of GEMs from the middle of the opaque emulsion layer into the Recovery Agent containing the Sub-Sampling Cleanup Mix. NOTE: This proportion can be adjusted to sub-sample required number of cells. For GEM volumes above 20 μl reduce Sub-Sampling Cleanup Mix volume by 0.7 μl for every 1 μl at step 2.3 and add that volume to remaining GEMs at step 2.7.
Using the same pipette, pipette-mix several times to break the emulsions.
Slowly remove 125 µl of Recovery Agent (pink) from the bottom according to the 10x user guide.

The Post GEM-RT Cleanup step with Dynabeads MyOne SILANE is done according to the v1 or v3 user guide for both fractions. NOTE: Each fraction will be treated as a separate sample and will follow the standard 10x protocol for the remaining steps. In addition to standard protocol, the sub-sample will go through long-read template preparation (Step 5).

Set up cDNA Amplification reactions for both fractions according to the user guide. NOTE: Identical reagents from Chromium Single Cell 5’ Library Construction Kit can be used for extra cDNA amplification (Kit dependent).

Adjust the PCR cycle numbers for each fraction based on cell number and carry out cleanup as per the 10x user guide. NOTE: In some experiments we have increased extension time to 3 min to allow efficient extension of longer cDNA fragments but we have not quantified the effect comparatively.

Carry out Gene Expression Library Construction for the main sample and the sub-sample separately according to the user guide. NOTE: Identical reagents from Chromium Single Cell 5’ Library Construction Kit can be used for extra library prep.
Sequencing is performed according to the 10x user guide recommendations.
Optimise cDNA PCR for long-read library preparation. %s %s In a thermocycler with heated lead, incubate as follows: %s %s
After 8 cycles take 5 µl out for analysis.
Repeat from Denaturation for 2 extra cycles and take 5 µl out and continue extra 2 cycles up to 14.
Quantify 2 µl of each sample taken from different cycles with Qubit dsDNA High Sensitivity assay.
Dilute each sample to 1 ng/µl and run on HSD5000 Tape.

The optimum number of cycles is that which yields at least 6 ng/µl cDNA without changing the peak distribution in the input cDNA. The following shows a comparison of input cDNA to PCR runs for 10-14 cycles on HSD5000 Tape. In this case all cycles met the peak distribution criteria and we chose 12 cycles for cDNA bulking. The position of the lower markers were adjusted to overlay peaks that migrate at different speeds due to varying amounts of salts.
Set up 5 PCR reactions and cycle them to the optimum number obtained from optimisation step.
Pool PCR reactions and add 0.6x SpriSelect beads and mix.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 min.
Place on the magnet until the solution clears.
Incubate for 2 min at room temperature.
Place the tube on the magnet until the solution clears.
Quantify 1 µl with Qubit dsDNA HS assay.
QC amplified cDNA by running a 1 ng/µl dilution on HSD5000 Tape.
Prepare library for Nanopore or PacBio sequencing according to the relevant protocol.
Remove the supernatant.
To wash add 200 µl freshly prepared 80% ethanol to the pellet. Wait 30 sec.
Remove the ethanol.
Repeat wash step for a total of 2 washes.
Centrifuge briefly and place on the magnet then remove any remaining ethanol.
Air dry for 2 min. Avoid overdrying (beads become cracked).
Remove from the magnet. Add 50 µl Buffer EB. Pipette mix 15x.
Transfer 49 µl of solution to a new tube, without disturbing the beads.
Process remaining GEMs (still in emultion layer) as per the 10x user guide.

After cleanup save 5-10 ng of the sub-sampled cDNA for long-read template preparation according to step 5 of this protocol. The remainder of the sub-sampled cDNA and main cDNA sample are utilised for short-read sequencing in steps 3 and 4 of this protocol. NOTE: Fractions for feature barcoding (if used) will be sequenced by short-read sequencing only.

Sequencing depth depends on the number of cells captured, sample source, required resolution and sequencing platform. UMI counts from analysis of Illumina reads can be used as a guide. With current chemistry 25% of Nanopore and 65% of PacBio reads can be assigned to cells.
